member.name: Alex Brewer
member.synopsis: Alex Brewer is the Liberal Democrat MP for North East Hampshire , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Railways: North East Hampshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, how much her Department has spent on rail infrastructure in North East Hampshire constituency in each of the last five years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Transport does not routinely record or report rail infrastructure expenditure by parliamentary constituency. Rail infrastructure investment is delivered primarily through Network Rail programmes and is allocated, monitored and published...

writtenQuestions.heading: Public Transport: North East Hampshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of a multimodal ticketing system on rural communities in North East Hampshire constituency reliant on rail-bus interchange.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the importance of good quality, reliable connections for passengers in rural communities who rely on interchange between different modes of transport.Under bus franchising and Enhanced Partnership arrangements, local transport au...

writtenQuestions.heading: Fuel Poverty
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what measures are in place to protect vulnerable households from fuel poverty following the increase in the energy price cap in July 2026.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Tackling affordability is this government’s number one priority. The Government acted at last year’s Budget by taking on average £150 of costs off energy bills, with those decisions now factored into bills for the years to come The recent rise in the pric...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Armed Forces and Veterans
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of the £15 billion increase in defence spending on (a) military housing and accommodation and (b) the Government's veterans strategy.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Defence Investment Plan confirms the commitment to £9.2 billion funding to deliver the recommendations of the Defence Housing Strategy and allows the Ministry of Defence (MOD) to press forward with the establishment of a new dedicated Defence Housing ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Fertilisers and Pesticides: Environment Protection
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what assessment her Department has made of the environmental impacts of (a) synthetic nitrogen fertilisers, (b) pesticides and (c) neonicotinoids; and what steps she is taking in respo
writtenQuestions.answerText: We know that the production of inorganic fertilisers is a significant source of greenhouse gas emissions and that the poor application of all fertilisers can have negative environmental impacts. We are supportive of all routes to more sustainable fertilis...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Maternity Services: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to ensure maternity services are able to respond to changes in demand and clinical practice, including (a) an increase in the average age of mothers at childbirt
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government and NHS England remain committed to ensuring safe, personalised, and evidence-based maternity care, grounded in informed choice. We will translate Baroness Amos’s National Maternity and Neonatal Investigation recommendations and Donna Ocken...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Maternity Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to help improve (a) maternity workplace cultures and (b) relationships between maternity staff and mothers.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Maternity and neonatal services have been supported to improve workplace culture through the Perinatal Culture and Leadership Programme (PCLP), which all 152 perinatal sites participated in between October 2022 and March 2025. The programme promoted compa...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Children: Social Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what assessment her Department has made of the of the impact of private equity investment on staffing in (a) children’s home (b) fostering services and (c) special educational needs schools.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Workforce data shows differences between staff in private and local authority homes, however the department does not have a breakdown for homes with private equity investment. For example, staff and managers in local authority homes are generally paid mor...

writtenQuestions.heading: Children: Social Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what assessment her Department has made of the impact of private equity investment on the performance of (a) children’s homes and (b) fostering services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Competition and Market Authority study into children’s social care did not find evidence that, on average, private provision was of lower quality than local authority provision. The study did not include a breakdown of private equity-backed investment...

writtenQuestions.heading: Parkinson's Disease: Nurses
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what estimate his Department have made of the number of specialist Parkinson’s nurses currently in post.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department does not hold a central estimate of the number of specialist Parkinson’s nurses employed in the National Health Service in England. Specialist Parkinson’s nurse posts are not recorded as a separate workforce category within national NHS wor...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Information Commissioner's Office: Fees and Charges
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment her Department has made of the impact of the Information Commissioner's Office's fee structure on small, self-employed data controllers such as registered childminders;
writtenQuestions.answerText: The data protection fees fund the ICO's statutory responsibilities under data protection and privacy and electronic communications legislation. There are three levels of fee payable based on organisational size. Micro organisations, including sole traders...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Veterans
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what steps his Department is taking to ensure veteran support meets the needs of underserved groups.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This government is committed to ensuring that all veterans, including those from groups which may have felt previously underrepresented, have easy access to support, when and where it is needed. That is why last year, we launched a new Veterans’ Strategy ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Armed Forces: Discharges
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what assessment his Department has made of the variation in (a) obtaining diagnosis (b) access to treatment during medical discharge.
writtenQuestions.answerText: A strong partnership exists between the Defence Medical Command (DMedC) and the NHS to ensure that personnel leaving the Armed Forces with ongoing medical needs experience a smooth transition of care, with continued access to a full suite of healthcare pr...

writtenQuestions.heading: Veterans: Rehabilitation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what steps his Department are taking to ensure that there is coordination between the Ministry of Defence, NHS and specialist providers to improve care pathways for veterans with complex rehabilitation needs.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government is committed to ensuring Armed Forces personnel and veterans receive the highest possible standard of care. A close partnership between Defence Medical Command (DMedC) and the NHS ensures that military and civilian healthcare services work...

writtenQuestions.heading: Armed Forces: Discharges
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, whether his Department has considered the potential benefit of commissioning an independent review of the medical discharge process that centres the lived experience of serving personnel and veterans.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the answer I gave on 1 July 2026 to Question 12263 to the hon. Member for Mid Buckinghamshire (Greg Smith).https://questions-statements.parliament.uk/written-questions/detail/2026-06-23/12263

writtenQuestions.heading: Armed Forces: Discharges
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what steps his Department are taking to ensure that medical discharge is a structured and supportive process; and what assessment his Department has made of veterans’ access to (a) appropriate rehabilitation (b)
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the answer I gave on 1 July 2026 to Question 12263 to the hon. Member for Mid Buckinghamshire (Greg Smith).https://questions-statements.parliament.uk/written-questions/detail/2026-06-23/12263

writtenQuestions.heading: Infected Blood Compensation Scheme
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, what steps the Government is taking to expedite compensation payments under the Infected Blood Compensation Scheme; and whether he will review the current timetable.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The delivery of compensation is a matter for the Infected Blood Compensation Authority (IBCA). The IBCA Framework Document, published in March 2025, sets out the timelines agreed between IBCA and Cabinet Office; namely for the bulk of infected people to b...

writtenQuestions.heading: Research and Development Tax Credit
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what the average processing time is for HMRC Research and Development tax relief claims; and what steps she is taking to reduce that time.
writtenQuestions.answerText: HMRC seeks to balance its compliance efforts with the need to pay legitimate claims promptly. HMRC aims to process 85% of payable Research and Development (R&D) tax credit claims within 40 days. The 85% aim acknowledges that certain circumstances will...

writtenQuestions.heading: Gynaecology: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what proportion of women with long-term gynaecological conditions are able to see a healthcare professional regularly to receive both physical and mental health support for their condition.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This information is not held centrally. We are committed to cutting waiting lists and improving waiting times across the board, ensuring patients get the care they need sooner.The Renewed Women’s Health Strategy for England, published in April 2026, repre...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Retail Trade: Urban Areas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what assessment his Department has made of the potential benefit of giving councils the power to designate Independent Shop Zones.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government recognises the important role that independent shops play in supporting vibrant, diverse and resilient high streets. Local authorities already have a range of tools to shape their town centres and support local businesses, including throug...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government
